entries.title: Pointe Group Overview
sampleQuestions: What is a Pointe Group?
Why should I join a group?
How large are Pointe Groups?
Where do Pointe Groups meet?
requiredFacts: A Pointe Group is a group of approximately 10 to 60 or more people.
Pointe Groups meet regularly to fellowship, study God's Word, build relationships, and grow together.
Groups may meet on CrossPointe's campus or in people's homes.
CrossPointe encourages everyone to become involved in a Pointe Group.

entries.title: Types of Pointe Groups and Intended Audiences
sampleQuestions: What kinds of groups do you have?
Do you have men's or women's groups?
Are there groups for my age or life stage?
Do you offer recovery or activity groups?
Can anyone attend every group?
requiredFacts: CrossPointe offers Sunday School, home, men's, women's, age- and life-stage, Bible-study, support or recovery, activity-based, and community groups.
Individual groups may be designed for a particular age range, gender, marital status, life stage, interest, area of study, or support need.
requiredActions: Use the group's current public description to identify its intended audience.
prohibitedClaims: Do not assume someone is or is not eligible when the directory does not make that clear.

entries.title: Current Pointe Group Directory
sampleQuestions: Where can I see all the groups?
What groups are currently meeting?
When does a particular group meet?
Where is a group located?
requiredFacts: The public Pointe Group directory is the approved live source for current group names, descriptions, intended audiences, meeting days, meeting times, public locations, and other changing information.
prohibitedClaims: Do not use an older newsletter, announcement, or social-media post when current directory information is available.

entries.title: Who May Join a Pointe Group
sampleQuestions: Do I need to be a member to join a group?
Are new people welcome?
Can anyone join a Pointe Group?
Is the group already full?
requiredFacts: Pointe Groups are open to everyone, and CrossPointe membership is not required.
Individual groups may be intended for a particular audience.
Pointe Groups are always accepting new people.
styleRules: Use welcoming language and avoid making someone feel that they are intruding on an established group.

entries.title: How to Join a Pointe Group
sampleQuestions: How do I join a group?
Can I just show up?
What does Request to Join do?
Will the group leader contact me?
requiredFacts: Someone may simply attend a publicly listed group meeting.
Someone may also select Request to Join in the online directory.
Request to Join notifies the group leader that the person would like to be contacted.

approvedLinks.label: Find and Join a Pointe Group

prohibitedBehaviors: Do not collect the person's name, phone number, email address, or other contact information in chat.

entries.title: Pointe Group Meeting Schedules and Seasons
sampleQuestions: How often do groups meet?
Do groups meet every week?
Do Pointe Groups meet during summer?
Is my group meeting this week?
requiredFacts: Each Pointe Group establishes its own schedule.
Sunday and Wednesday groups normally meet weekly.
Other groups may meet weekly, every other week, monthly, or on another schedule.
Some groups meet year-round, while others take a summer break.
requiredActions: Confirm a specific group's current schedule through the public directory.
prohibitedClaims: Do not assume a group is meeting because it met at that time previously.

entries.title: Home-Group Address and Leader Privacy
sampleQuestions: What is the home group's street address?
Can you give me the leader's phone number?
Where exactly does this home group meet?
How do I get directions to the host's house?
requiredFacts: For a home group, the group leader provides the private address and meeting instructions after someone requests to join.
prohibitedInformation: personal information about hosts or participants
directions not published for general use
requiredActions: Direct the person to Request to Join through the Pointe Group directory.

entries.title: Pointe Group Childcare
sampleQuestions: Is childcare available at Pointe Groups?
Can I bring my children?
Who should I ask about childcare?
requiredFacts: Childcare availability varies by group.
requiredActions: Use the current group description when childcare is listed.
Otherwise, direct the family to Request to Join or contact the group leader through the directory.
prohibitedClaims: Do not promise childcare unless the current group description specifically confirms it.

entries.title: Pointe Group Costs and Materials
sampleQuestions: Does it cost money to join a group?
Do I need to buy a book?
Are group activities free?
Will I need to pay for a meal?
requiredFacts: Pointe Groups are free to join.
Some groups may ask participants to purchase a book or study material, pay for an optional activity, or contribute to a group-specific expense.
Additional costs are not characteristic of every group.
prohibitedClaims: Do not promise that every book, meal, trip, or activity is free unless the current group description confirms it.

entries.title: What to Expect at a Pointe Group
sampleQuestions: What happens at a Pointe Group?
Will there be food?
Is it a Bible study?
What are home groups like?
What happens in Sunday or Wednesday groups?
requiredFacts: Each Pointe Group is different.
Home groups commonly include a meal, fellowship, and teaching or Bible study.
Some community groups may include a meal or activity, fellowship, and possible teaching or Bible study.
Sunday and Wednesday groups typically include fellowship and teaching or Bible study without a regular meal.
styleRules: Describe these as common patterns rather than guarantees.
Use the current group description as the authority for a specific group.

entries.title: Finding a Pointe Group That Fits
sampleQuestions: Can you help me find a group?
Which group fits my schedule?
Is there a group for my life stage?
Do any groups offer childcare?
requiredActions: Search or direct the person to the current Pointe Group directory.
prohibitedBehaviors: Do not ask for sensitive personal information.
Do not decide that someone belongs in a support or recovery group.

entries.title: When No Pointe Group Appears to Fit
sampleQuestions: I can't find a group that works for me.
There are no groups near me.
None of the schedules fit.
What if I can't find the right group?
requiredActions: Encourage the person to contact the church office by email at info@crosspointe.tv or by calling or texting 405-374-4740.
prohibitedClaims: Do not claim that no appropriate group exists merely because Wayfinder could not find one.

entries.title: Starting or Leading a Pointe Group
sampleQuestions: How do I start a Pointe Group?
Can I become a group leader?
Who approves new groups?
What training is required for leaders?
requiredFacts: Someone interested in starting or leading a Pointe Group should contact the church office to schedule a conversation with a pastor.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

prohibitedClaims: Do not describe internal leadership approval, training, or qualification requirements unless specifically approved later.

entries.title: Pointe Group Oversight and Contact
sampleQuestions: Who oversees Pointe Groups?
How can I contact Mike Lewis about groups?
Who can answer my group question?
requiredFacts: Mike Lewis, CrossPointe's Worship Pastor, oversees Pointe Groups.
prohibitedClaims: Do not expose Mike's direct phone number or email address.
